key_insight: A lightweight router can adapt Hebbian fast-memory behavior inside Vision Transformers, improving few-shot recognition over fixed Hebbian updates.
engineering_relevance: Adaptive memory control offers a practical way to make transformer-based recognition systems more robust and efficient when labeled data are scarce.
method: MLP router modulates memory placement, update strength, and retention.
Compared Adaptive Placement, Adaptive Plasticity, and Fully Adaptive Hebbian Routing.
Tested on ViT-Small, DeiT-Small, and Swin-Tiny in 5-way few-shot settings.
evidence_signal: Across Omniglot, CIFAR-FS, and cross-domain transfer, adaptive variants consistently improved accuracy; on Swin, Fully Adaptive Routing reached 96.94% and reduced inference time from 16.51 ms to 14.05 ms.
watch_item: Gains depend on the router design and memory location, so added adaptivity may increase implementation complexity and require careful tuning.
paper_title: Adaptive Hebbian Memory Routing in Vision Transformers for Few-Shot Learning
